However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|5th Dynasty of Egypt|de|5. Dynastie|qq=draft}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|de|Abusir|qq=draft}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Ghurab|de|Abu Ghurab|qq=draft}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|de|Tempel von Derr|qq=draft}}''' - The '''{{ill|Great Temple of Abu Simbel|de|Tempel von Abu Simbel|qq=draft}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|de|Thutmosis III.|qq=draft}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|de|Kartusche (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ptah|de|Ptah|qq=draft}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Amentet|de|Amentet|qq=draft}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|de|Hathor|qq=draft}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|de|Nefertari (19. Dynastie)|qq=draft}}''', '''{{ill|QV66|de|QV66|qq=draft}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|de|Thelema|qq=draft}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|de|Aleister Crowley|qq=draft}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|de|Liber AL vel Legis|qq=draft}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste ägyptischer Götter|qq=draft}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]]

{{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Unguent cone|de|Salbkegel|qq=draft}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|de|Louvre|qq=draft}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|22nd Dynasty of Egypt|de|22. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|qq=draft}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=draft}}) of {{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|qq=draft}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|qq=draft}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|lt=Uraeus serpent|de|Uräusschlange (Symbol)|qq=draft}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|de|Totentempel des Sethos I. (Abydos)|qq=draft}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|qq=draft}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|qq=draft}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|de|Echnaton|qq=draft}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|5th Dynasty of Egypt|de|5. Dynastie|qq=draft}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|de|Abusir|qq=draft}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Ghurab|de|Abu Ghurab|qq=draft}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|de|Tempel von Derr|qq=draft}}''' - The '''{{ill|Great Temple of Abu Simbel|de|Tempel von Abu Simbel|qq=draft}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|de|Thutmosis III.|qq=draft}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|de|Kartusche (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ptah|de|Ptah|qq=draft}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Amentet|de|Amentet|qq=draft}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|de|Hathor|qq=draft}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|de|Nefertari (19. Dynastie)|qq=draft}}''', '''{{ill|QV66|de|QV66|qq=draft}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|de|Thelema|qq=draft}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|de|Aleister Crowley|qq=draft}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|de|Liber AL vel Legis|qq=draft}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste ägyptischer Götter|qq=draft}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}}

However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|5th Dynasty of Egypt|de|5. Dynastie|qq=draft}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|de|Abusir|qq=draft}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Ghurab|de|Abu Ghurab|qq=draft}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|de|Tempel von Derr|qq=draft}}''' - The '''{{ill|Great Temple of Abu Simbel|de|Tempel von Abu Simbel|qq=draft}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|de|Thutmosis III.|qq=draft}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|de|Kartusche (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ptah|de|Ptah|qq=draft}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Amentet|de|Amentet|qq=draft}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|de|Hathor|qq=draft}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|de|Nefertari (19. Dynastie)|qq=draft}}''', '''{{ill|QV66|de|QV66|qq=draft}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|de|Thelema|qq=draft}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|de|Aleister Crowley|qq=draft}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|de|Liber AL vel Legis|qq=draft}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste ägyptischer Götter|qq=draft}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages without German translation box]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}}

{{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Unguent cone|de|Salbkegel|qq=draft}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|de|Louvre|qq=draft}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|22nd Dynasty of Egypt|de|22. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|qq=draft}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=draft}}) of {{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|qq=draft}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|qq=draft}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|lt=Uraeus serpent|de|Uräusschlange (Symbol)|qq=draft}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|de|Totentempel des Sethos I. (Abydos)|qq=draft}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|qq=draft}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|qq=draft}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|de|Echnaton|qq=draft}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|5th Dynasty of Egypt|de|5. Dynastie|qq=draft}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|de|Abusir|qq=draft}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Ghurab|de|Abu Ghurab|qq=draft}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|de|Tempel von Derr|qq=draft}}''' - The '''{{ill|Great Temple of Abu Simbel|de|Tempel von Abu Simbel|qq=draft}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|de|Thutmosis III.|qq=draft}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|de|Kartusche (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ptah|de|Ptah|qq=draft}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Amentet|de|Amentet|qq=draft}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|de|Hathor|qq=draft}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|de|Nefertari (19. Dynastie)|qq=draft}}''', '''{{ill|QV66|de|QV66|qq=draft}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|de|Thelema|qq=draft}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|de|Aleister Crowley|qq=draft}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|de|Liber AL vel Legis|qq=draft}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste ägyptischer Götter|qq=draft}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}}

However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|12=draft}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|12=draft}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|5th Dynasty of Egypt|de|5. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=draft}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|de|Abusir|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Ghurab|de|Abu Ghurab|qq=draft|12=draft}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|de|Tempel von Derr|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' - The '''{{ill|Great Temple of Abu Simbel|de|Tempel von Abu Simbel|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|de|Thutmosis III.|qq=draft|12=draft}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|de|Kartusche (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ptah|de|Ptah|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Amentet|de|Amentet|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|de|Hathor|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|de|Nefertari (19. Dynastie)|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', '''{{ill|QV66|de|QV66|qq=draft|12=draft}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|de|Thelema|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|de|Aleister Crowley|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|de|Liber AL vel Legis|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste ägyptischer Götter|qq=draft|12=draft}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]]

{{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|12=draft}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Unguent cone|de|Salbkegel|qq=draft|12=draft}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|de|Louvre|qq=draft|12=draft}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|22nd Dynasty of Egypt|de|22. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=draft}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|qq=draft|12=draft}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=draft|12=draft}}) of {{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|qq=draft|12=draft}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|lt=Uraeus serpent|de|Uräusschlange (Symbol)|qq=draft|12=draft}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|de|Totentempel des Sethos I. (Abydos)|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft|12=draft}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|qq=draft|12=draft}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|12=draft}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|qq=draft|12=draft}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|de|Echnaton|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|12=draft}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|12=draft}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|5th Dynasty of Egypt|de|5. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=draft}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|de|Abusir|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Ghurab|de|Abu Ghurab|qq=draft|12=draft}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|de|Tempel von Derr|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' - The '''{{ill|Great Temple of Abu Simbel|de|Tempel von Abu Simbel|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|de|Thutmosis III.|qq=draft|12=draft}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|de|Kartusche (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ptah|de|Ptah|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Amentet|de|Amentet|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|de|Hathor|qq=draft|12=draft}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|de|Nefertari (19. Dynastie)|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', '''{{ill|QV66|de|QV66|qq=draft|12=draft}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|de|Thelema|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|de|Aleister Crowley|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|de|Liber AL vel Legis|qq=draft|12=draft}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste ägyptischer Götter|qq=draft|12=draft}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]]

Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pharaoh|qq=}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|de|Echnaton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akhenaten|qq=}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|5th Dynasty of Egypt|de|5. Dynastie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|de|Abusir|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abusir|qq=}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Ghurab|de|Abu Ghurab|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abu Ghurab|qq=}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|de|Tempel von Derr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Derr|qq=}}''' - The '''{{ill|Great Temple of Abu Simbel|de|Tempel von Abu Simbel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Temple of Abu Simbel|qq=}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesses II|qq=}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|de|Thutmosis III.|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thutmose III|qq=}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|de|Kartusche (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|de|Ptah|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ptah|qq=}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Amentet|de|Amentet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amentet|qq=}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|de|Hathor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hathor|qq=}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|de|Nefertari (19. Dynastie)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|qq=}}''', '''{{ill|QV66|de|QV66|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/QV66|qq=}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|de|Thelema|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thelema|qq=}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|de|Aleister Crowley|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aleister Crowley|qq=}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|de|Liber AL vel Legis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Book of the Law|qq=}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste ägyptischer Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]]

{{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}}-{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus|qq=}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Unguent cone|de|Salbkegel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unguent cone|qq=}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|de|Louvre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Louvre|qq=}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|22nd Dynasty of Egypt|de|22. Dynastie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/22nd Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian mythology|qq=}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of {{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|lt=Uraeus serpent|de|Uräusschlange (Symbol)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uraeus|qq=}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|de|Totentempel des Sethos I. (Abydos)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|qq=}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edfu|qq=}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus|qq=}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pharaoh|qq=}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|de|Echnaton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akhenaten|qq=}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|5th Dynasty of Egypt|de|5. Dynastie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|de|Abusir|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abusir|qq=}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Ghurab|de|Abu Ghurab|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abu Ghurab|qq=}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|de|Tempel von Derr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Derr|qq=}}''' - The '''{{ill|Great Temple of Abu Simbel|de|Tempel von Abu Simbel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Temple of Abu Simbel|qq=}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesses II|qq=}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|de|Thutmosis III.|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thutmose III|qq=}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|de|Kartusche (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|de|Ptah|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ptah|qq=}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Amentet|de|Amentet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amentet|qq=}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|de|Hathor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hathor|qq=}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|de|Nefertari (19. Dynastie)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|qq=}}''', '''{{ill|QV66|de|QV66|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/QV66|qq=}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|de|Thelema|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thelema|qq=}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|de|Aleister Crowley|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aleister Crowley|qq=}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|de|Liber AL vel Legis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Book of the Law|qq=}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste ägyptischer Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]]

{{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''[[:en:Ra|Ra]]-[[:en:Horus|Horus]], Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with [[:en:Head cone|Unguent cone]] before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the [[:en:Louvre|Louvre]], ca. 900 BCE, [[:en:Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|22nd Dynasty of Egypt]]) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]]. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of [[:en:Ra|Ra]] and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''[[:en:Uraeus|Uraeus serpent]]'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * [[:en:List of Egyptian deities|List of Egyptian deities]] == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Re-Harachte]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]]

{{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''[[:en:Ra|Ra]]-[[:en:Horus|Horus]], Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with [[:en:Head cone|Unguent cone]] before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the [[:en:Louvre|Louvre]], ca. 900 BCE, [[:en:Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|22nd Dynasty of Egypt]]) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]]. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of [[:en:Ra|Ra]] and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''[[:en:Uraeus|Uraeus serpent]]'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * [[:en:List of Egyptian deities|List of Egyptian deities]] == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]]

''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''[[:en:Ra|Ra]]-[[:en:Horus|Horus]], Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with [[:en:Head cone|Unguent cone]] before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the [[:en:Louvre|Louvre]], ca. 900 BCE, [[:en:Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|22nd Dynasty of Egypt]]) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]]. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of [[:en:Ra|Ra]] and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''[[:en:Uraeus|Uraeus serpent]]'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * [[:en:List of Egyptian deities|List of Egyptian deities]] == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[en:draft:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[Category:Falkengottheit]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]]

This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of [[:en:Ra|Ra]] and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''[[:en:Uraeus|Uraeus serpent]]'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * [[:en:List of Egyptian deities|List of Egyptian deities]] == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Male deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Hawk deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[Category:Falkengottheit]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * [[:en:List of Egyptian deities|List of Egyptian deities]] == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Ra-Horakhty]] [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[sn:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]
