This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of [[:en:Ra|Ra]] and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''[[:en:Uraeus|Uraeus serpent]]'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * [[:en:List of Egyptian deities|List of Egyptian deities]] == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] {{wikidata link|Q1418931}} [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * [[:en:List of Egyptian deities|List of Egyptian deities]] == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' ''[[:en:Ra|Ra]]-[[:en:Horus|Horus]], Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with [[:en:Head cone|Unguent cone]] before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the [[:en:Louvre|Louvre]], ca. 900 BCE, [[:en:Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|22nd Dynasty of Egypt]]) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]]. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of [[:en:Ra|Ra]] and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''[[:en:Uraeus|Uraeus serpent]]'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[sn:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] {{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''[[:en:Ra|Ra]]-[[:en:Horus|Horus]], Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with [[:en:Head cone|Unguent cone]] before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the [[:en:Louvre|Louvre]], ca. 900 BCE, [[:en:Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|22nd Dynasty of Egypt]]) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]]. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of [[:en:Ra|Ra]] and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''[[:en:Uraeus|Uraeus serpent]]'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''[[:en:Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)]]''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]], '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god [[:en:Horus|Horus]].'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. [[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]] '''[[:en:Akhenaten|Akhenaten]]''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''[[:en:Temple of Derr|Temple of Derr]]''' - The '''[[:en:Abu Simbel|Great Temple of Abu Simbel]]''', built by '''[[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]]''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|[[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]] in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''[[:en:Cartouche|cartouche (Ancient Egypt)]]''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ptah|Ptah]]''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''[[:en:Imentet|Amentet]]''' in the form of '''[[:en:Hathor|Hathor]]''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''[[:en:Nefertari|Nefertari (19th Dynasty)]]''', '''[[:en:Tomb of Nefertari|QV66]]''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''[[:en:Thelema|Thelema]]''', a '''new religious movement founded by [[:en:Aleister Crowley|Aleister Crowley]]''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''[[:en:The Book of the Law|The Book of the Law]]''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * [[:en:List of Egyptian deities|List of Egyptian deities]] == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] {{wikidata link|Q1418931}} [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ([[:en:Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty of Egypt]]), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''[[:en:Abusir|Abusir]]''' ('''[[:en:Abu Gorab|Abu Ghurab]]'''). ''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra}}-{{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Head cone|en|Head cone|WD=Q2213984|lt=Unguent cone}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|en|Louvre|WD=Q19675|lt=Louvre}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q737629|lt=22nd Dynasty of Egypt}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|en|Uraeus|WD=Q756990|lt=Uraeus serpent}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|en|Akhenaten|WD=Q81794|lt=Akhenaten}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty of Egypt}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|en|Abusir|WD=Q336098|lt=Abusir}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Gorab|en|Abu Gorab|WD=Q2038886|lt=Abu Ghurab}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|en|Temple of Derr|WD=Q7698721|lt=Temple of Derr}}''' - The '''{{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Great Temple of Abu Simbel}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|Cartouche|en|Cartouche|WD=Q224104|lt=cartouche (Ancient Egypt)}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Imentet|en|Imentet|WD=Q262099|lt=Amentet}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|WD=Q133343|lt=Hathor}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari|en|Nefertari|WD=Q210535|lt=Nefertari (19th Dynasty)}}''', '''{{ill|Tomb of Nefertari|en|Tomb of Nefertari|WD=Q926366|lt=QV66}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|en|Thelema|WD=Q745955|lt=Thelema}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|en|Aleister Crowley|WD=Q172684|lt=Aleister Crowley}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|en|The Book of the Law|WD=Q1169632|lt=The Book of the Law}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|WD=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian deities}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] {{wikidata link|Q1418931}} [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra}}-{{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Head cone|en|Head cone|WD=Q2213984|lt=Unguent cone}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|en|Louvre|WD=Q19675|lt=Louvre}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q737629|lt=22nd Dynasty of Egypt}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|en|Uraeus|WD=Q756990|lt=Uraeus serpent}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|en|Akhenaten|WD=Q81794|lt=Akhenaten}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty of Egypt}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|en|Abusir|WD=Q336098|lt=Abusir}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Gorab|en|Abu Gorab|WD=Q2038886|lt=Abu Ghurab}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|en|Temple of Derr|WD=Q7698721|lt=Temple of Derr}}''' - The '''{{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Great Temple of Abu Simbel}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|Cartouche|en|Cartouche|WD=Q224104|lt=cartouche (Ancient Egypt)}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Imentet|en|Imentet|WD=Q262099|lt=Amentet}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|WD=Q133343|lt=Hathor}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari|en|Nefertari|WD=Q210535|lt=Nefertari (19th Dynasty)}}''', '''{{ill|Tomb of Nefertari|en|Tomb of Nefertari|WD=Q926366|lt=QV66}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|en|Thelema|WD=Q745955|lt=Thelema}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|en|Aleister Crowley|WD=Q172684|lt=Aleister Crowley}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|en|The Book of the Law|WD=Q1169632|lt=The Book of the Law}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|WD=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian deities}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] {{wikidata link|Q1418931}} [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|en|Akhenaten|WD=Q81794|lt=Akhenaten}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty of Egypt}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|en|Abusir|WD=Q336098|lt=Abusir}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Gorab|en|Abu Gorab|WD=Q2038886|lt=Abu Ghurab}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|en|Temple of Derr|WD=Q7698721|lt=Temple of Derr}}''' - The '''{{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Great Temple of Abu Simbel}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|Cartouche|en|Cartouche|WD=Q224104|lt=cartouche (Ancient Egypt)}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Imentet|en|Imentet|WD=Q262099|lt=Amentet}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|WD=Q133343|lt=Hathor}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari|en|Nefertari|WD=Q210535|lt=Nefertari (19th Dynasty)}}''', '''{{ill|Tomb of Nefertari|en|Tomb of Nefertari|WD=Q926366|lt=QV66}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|en|Thelema|WD=Q745955|lt=Thelema}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|en|Aleister Crowley|WD=Q172684|lt=Aleister Crowley}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|en|The Book of the Law|WD=Q1169632|lt=The Book of the Law}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|WD=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian deities}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] {{wikidata link|Q1418931}} [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

[[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[co:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[co:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[sn:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] {{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra}}-{{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Head cone|en|Head cone|WD=Q2213984|lt=Unguent cone}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|en|Louvre|WD=Q19675|lt=Louvre}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q737629|lt=22nd Dynasty of Egypt}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|en|Uraeus|WD=Q756990|lt=Uraeus serpent}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|en|Akhenaten|WD=Q81794|lt=Akhenaten}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty of Egypt}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|en|Abusir|WD=Q336098|lt=Abusir}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Gorab|en|Abu Gorab|WD=Q2038886|lt=Abu Ghurab}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|en|Temple of Derr|WD=Q7698721|lt=Temple of Derr}}''' - The '''{{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Great Temple of Abu Simbel}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|Cartouche|en|Cartouche|WD=Q224104|lt=cartouche (Ancient Egypt)}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Imentet|en|Imentet|WD=Q262099|lt=Amentet}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|WD=Q133343|lt=Hathor}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari|en|Nefertari|WD=Q210535|lt=Nefertari (19th Dynasty)}}''', '''{{ill|Tomb of Nefertari|en|Tomb of Nefertari|WD=Q926366|lt=QV66}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|en|Thelema|WD=Q745955|lt=Thelema}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|en|Aleister Crowley|WD=Q172684|lt=Aleister Crowley}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|en|The Book of the Law|WD=Q1169632|lt=The Book of the Law}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|WD=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian deities}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] {{wikidata link|Q1418931}} [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|en|Akhenaten|WD=Q81794|lt=Akhenaten}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty of Egypt}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|en|Abusir|WD=Q336098|lt=Abusir}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Gorab|en|Abu Gorab|WD=Q2038886|lt=Abu Ghurab}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|en|Temple of Derr|WD=Q7698721|lt=Temple of Derr}}''' - The '''{{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Great Temple of Abu Simbel}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|Cartouche|en|Cartouche|WD=Q224104|lt=cartouche (Ancient Egypt)}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Imentet|en|Imentet|WD=Q262099|lt=Amentet}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|WD=Q133343|lt=Hathor}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari|en|Nefertari|WD=Q210535|lt=Nefertari (19th Dynasty)}}''', '''{{ill|Tomb of Nefertari|en|Tomb of Nefertari|WD=Q926366|ltq=QV66}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|en|Thelema|WD=Q745955|lt=Thelema}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|en|Aleister Crowley|WD=Q172684|lt=Aleister Crowley}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|en|The Book of the Law|WD=Q1169632|lt=The Book of the Law}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|WD=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian deities}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] {{wikidata link|Q1418931}} [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[co:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[co:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[sn:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] {{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}}-{{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Head cone|en|Head cone|WD=Q2213984|lt=Unguent cone|1=Head cone}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|en|Louvre|WD=Q19675|lt=Louvre|1=Louvre}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q737629|lt=22nd Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|en|Uraeus|WD=Q756990|lt=Uraeus serpent|1=Uraeus}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|1=Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu|1=Edfu}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh|1=Pharaoh}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|en|Akhenaten|WD=Q81794|lt=Akhenaten|1=Akhenaten}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fifth Dynasty of Egypt}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|en|Abusir|WD=Q336098|lt=Abusir|1=Abusir}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Gorab|en|Abu Gorab|WD=Q2038886|lt=Abu Ghurab|1=Abu Gorab}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|en|Temple of Derr|WD=Q7698721|lt=Temple of Derr|1=Temple of Derr}}''' - The '''{{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Great Temple of Abu Simbel|1=Abu Simbel}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|Cartouche|en|Cartouche|WD=Q224104|lt=cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|1=Cartouche}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Imentet|en|Imentet|WD=Q262099|lt=Amentet|1=Imentet}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|WD=Q133343|lt=Hathor|1=Hathor}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari|en|Nefertari|WD=Q210535|lt=Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|1=Nefertari}}''', '''{{ill|Tomb of Nefertari|en|Tomb of Nefertari|WD=Q926366|ltq=QV66|1=Tomb of Nefertari}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|en|Thelema|WD=Q745955|lt=Thelema|1=Thelema}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|en|Aleister Crowley|WD=Q172684|lt=Aleister Crowley|1=Aleister Crowley}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|en|The Book of the Law|WD=Q1169632|lt=The Book of the Law|1=The Book of the Law}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|WD=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian deities|1=List of Egyptian deities}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Special edition, Scherz, Bern, 1998, ISBN 3-502-16430-4, p. 91. * '''Rolf Felde''': ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' 2nd expanded and improved edition, R. Felde Publishing, Wiesbaden, 1995, pp. 50–51. * '''Hans Bonnet''': ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte.'' Nikol, Hamburg, 2000, ISBN 3-937872-08-6, pp. 269, 629–630. == External links == {{Commonscat|Ra-Horakhty|Re-Harakhte}} == Notes == {{translated page|de|Re-Harachte|version=234657501|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Re-Harachte]] [[it:Ra-Horakhti]] [[es:Ra-Horajty]] [[ar:%D8%B1%D8%B9-%D8%AD%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%AE%D8%AA%D9%8A]] [[uk:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%93%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D1%96]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B0-%D0%A5%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%85%D1%82%D0%B5]] [[ro:Ra-horakhty]] [[pl:Re-Horachte]] [[hr:Ra-Harakhte]] [[fr:R%C3%AA-Horakhty]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]] [[ca:Re-Horakhty]] {{Commons category|Ra-Horakhty}} [[en:Re-Hor-achti]] [[fi:Ra-Horakhty]] [[fr:Rê-Horakhty]] [[ru:Ра-Хорахте]] [[sh:Ra-Harakhte]] [[so:Waaqiya (diin)]] [[sv:Ra-Harakhte]] [[uk:Ра-Горахті]] {{wikidata link|Q1418931}} [[Category:Animal deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Egyptian deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Falcon deities]] [[Category:Falcon Gods]] [[Category:Gods]] [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|en|Uraeus|WD=Q756990|lt=Uraeus serpent|1=Uraeus}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|1=Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu|1=Edfu}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh|1=Pharaoh}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|en|Akhenaten|WD=Q81794|lt=Akhenaten|1=Akhenaten}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fifth Dynasty of Egypt}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|en|Abusir|WD=Q336098|lt=Abusir|1=Abusir}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Gorab|en|Abu Gorab|WD=Q2038886|lt=Abu Ghurab|1=Abu Gorab}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|en|Temple of Derr|WD=Q7698721|lt=Temple of Derr|1=Temple of Derr}}''' - The '''{{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Great Temple of Abu Simbel|1=Abu Simbel}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|Cartouche|en|Cartouche|WD=Q224104|lt=cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|1=Cartouche}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Imentet|en|Imentet|WD=Q262099|lt=Amentet|1=Imentet}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|WD=Q133343|lt=Hathor|1=Hathor}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari|en|Nefertari|WD=Q210535|lt=Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|1=Nefertari}}''', '''{{ill|Tomb of Nefertari|en|Tomb of Nefertari|WD=Q926366|ltq=QV66|1=Tomb of Nefertari}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|en|Thelema|WD=Q745955|lt=Thelema|1=Thelema}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|en|Aleister Crowley|WD=Q172684|lt=Aleister Crowley|1=Aleister Crowley}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|en|The Book of the Law|WD=Q1169632|lt=The Book of the Law|1=The Book of the Law}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|WD=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian deities|1=List of Egyptian deities}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[co:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[co:commons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] [[ar:رع-حوراختي]][[ca:Re-Horakhty]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Ra-Horakhty]][[de:Re-Harachte]][[en:Re-Hor-achti]][[es:Ra-Horajty]][[fi:Ra-Horakhty]][[fr:Rê-Horakhty]][[hr:Ra-Harakhte]][[it:Ra-Horakhti]][[pl:Re-Horachte]][[ro:Ra-horakhty]][[ru:Ра-Хорахте]][[sh:Ra-Harakhte]][[so:Waaqiya (diin)]][[sv:Ra-Harakhte]][[uk:Ра-Горахті]][[ur:رع-حوراختی]] {{Infobox deity |IDEOGRAM = G9 |NAME-EXTENDED = Fully written out |NAME = ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ra(u)-heru-achti
''Rˁ(w)-ḥr(.w)-3ḫ.tj''
''{{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}}-{{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}, Dweller of the Horizon'' |IMAGE1 = Stele Ra-Horakhty Louvre E5789.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 180px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Priest Renpetmaa with {{ill|Head cone|en|Head cone|WD=Q2213984|lt=Unguent cone|1=Head cone}} before Re-Harakhte (Stele in the {{ill|Louvre|en|Louvre|WD=Q19675|lt=Louvre|1=Louvre}}, ca. 900 BCE, {{ill|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q737629|lt=22nd Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt}}) }} '''Re-Harakhte''' (also spelled '''Re-Horakhty''', '''Re-Hor-achti''') is a {{ill|solar deity|de|Sonnengott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar deity|qq=}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}}. This god emerged through the fusion ({{ill|Syncretism (Egyptology)|de|Synkretismus (Ägyptologie)|1=Syncretism (Egyptology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism (Egyptology)|qq=}}) of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}} and {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=}}. == Depiction == Re-Harakhte is typically portrayed as a '''standing or seated man''' with '''red skin''' and a '''falcon head'''. On his head, he wears the '''{{ill|solar disk|de|Sonnenscheibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar disk|qq=}}''', encircled by the '''{{ill|Uraeus|en|Uraeus|WD=Q756990|lt=Uraeus serpent|1=Uraeus}}'''. Other representations depict him as a '''human with a ram's head''' and a '''solar disk''', such as in the '''{{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|1=Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}''', or as having a '''lion, cat, or falcon head with ram horns and a solar disk''', particularly in '''({{ill|Hibis|de|Hibis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibis|qq=}}).''' == Meaning == Re-Harakhte describes Ra as the '''rising sun in the east.''' It is the actual name of the god '''Ra of Heliopolis.'''Rolf Felde: ''Ägyptische Gottheiten.'' Wiesbaden, 1995, p. 51. As the '''Heliopolitan sun god''', he is highlighted as the '''"ruler of the world."''' In {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu|1=Edfu}}, '''Re-Harakhte of Heliopolis''' was regarded as '''identical to the god {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}.'''M. Lurker: ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.'' Bern, 1998, p. 91. {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh|1=Pharaoh}} '''{{ill|Akhenaten|en|Akhenaten|WD=Q81794|lt=Akhenaten|1=Akhenaten}}''' called himself the '''"High Priest of Re-Harakhte"''', and at the start of his reign, he depicted the god in his traditional form. However, in '''the first so-called "{{ill|Aten|lt=Didactic name of Aten|de|Aton#Der lehrhafte Name des Aton|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aten|qq=}}"''' (Years 4–8 of his reign), he described '''Aten''' as '''Re-Harakhte''': *"Long live the '''Horizon Horus''' (Re-Harakhte), who rejoices in the Light Land (Horizon)."* == Cult and {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == As early as '''2445 BCE''' ({{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fifth Dynasty of Egypt}}), the '''fusion form of these two gods''' appeared in the '''{{ill|Sun Temple of Nyuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Nyuserre|qq=}}''' at '''{{ill|Abusir|en|Abusir|WD=Q336098|lt=Abusir|1=Abusir}}''' ('''{{ill|Abu Gorab|en|Abu Gorab|WD=Q2038886|lt=Abu Ghurab|1=Abu Gorab}}'''). Re-Harakhte was also worshiped in temples at: * '''Wadi es-Sebua''' * '''{{ill|Amada (Nubian village)|de|Amada (nubisches Dorf)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amada (Nubian village)|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Temple of Derr|en|Temple of Derr|WD=Q7698721|lt=Temple of Derr|1=Temple of Derr}}''' - The '''{{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Great Temple of Abu Simbel|1=Abu Simbel}}''', built by '''{{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}''' Amada relief of Thutmose III.jpg|{{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}} in the temple of '''Amada''' before '''Re-Harakhte''' - 001 Torino Museo Egizio geroglifici su pietra.jpg|Cartouches of '''Aten''' (First didactic name of Aten) mentioning '''Re-Harakhte''' in the first '''{{ill|Cartouche|en|Cartouche|WD=Q224104|lt=cartouche (Ancient Egypt)|1=Cartouche}}''' (read from right to left) Großer Tempel (Abu Simbel) 18.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''' Flickr - archer10 (Dennis) - Egypt-10C-024.jpg|'''Great Temple of Abu Simbel''', sanctuary with '''Re-Harakhte, Ramesses II, {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}''' (from right to left) Maler der Grabkammer der Nefertari 001.jpg|'''{{ill|Imentet|en|Imentet|WD=Q262099|lt=Amentet|1=Imentet}}''' in the form of '''{{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|WD=Q133343|lt=Hathor|1=Hathor}}''' with '''Re-Harakhte''' (Tomb of '''{{ill|Nefertari|en|Nefertari|WD=Q210535|lt=Nefertari (19th Dynasty)|1=Nefertari}}''', '''{{ill|Tomb of Nefertari|en|Tomb of Nefertari|WD=Q926366|ltq=QV66|1=Tomb of Nefertari}}''') == Reception in the New Religious Movement Thelema == In '''{{ill|Thelema|en|Thelema|WD=Q745955|lt=Thelema|1=Thelema}}''', a '''new religious movement founded by {{ill|Aleister Crowley|en|Aleister Crowley|WD=Q172684|lt=Aleister Crowley|1=Aleister Crowley}}''', Re-Harakhte is known as '''"Ra-Hoor-Khuit"''' and represents '''one of the three central principles of Thelemic doctrine.''' "Ra-Hoor-Khuit" appears as the speaker of the '''third chapter''' of '''{{ill|The Book of the Law|en|The Book of the Law|WD=Q1169632|lt=The Book of the Law|1=The Book of the Law}}''', the '''sacred text of Thelema.'''Rodney Orpheus: ''Abrahadabra: Understanding Aleister Crowley's Thelemic Magick.'' Weiser, Boston MA, 2005, ISBN 1-57863-326-5, pp. 33–44. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|WD=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian deities|1=List of Egyptian deities}} == References == * '''Manfred Lurker''': ''Lexikon der Götter und Symbole der alten Ägypter.''
